It seems almost essay-like. It's not that what it's saying is bad, but how it's saying it as a bit too formal. Also, a technical bug. At least for my testing, everytime I came back for a new day and sent a new message, it would respond to the previous message I said from the day before respondinfg to the new prompt. I also feel, in generaL the memory/recognition of past responses during an ongoing conversation is not there.

user_opinion_1: I don't feel lonely as I appreciate my free time alone and am very productive in both work and leisure, so I cherish the time I can spend alone.  Also, I appreciate my friends and family for the time that we spend together and recognize those moments as special and dear to me.  This means that I can appreciate life either way and not feel lonely even during times where I am literally alone.  If I was alone for an extended period of time, I would certainly miss people and I would have had a slightly different experience, but I'm not sure I would see the bot as a proxy for true proximity to others.
user_opinion_2: The bot overall worked well for me, but I could definitely tell it was an AI from the turn-based nature of the conversation, the speed at which it answered, and the agreeable optimism to everything I said.  I don't mind talking to AI, and in fact I do enjoy it, but talking with a Language Model is no replacement for real socialization.  There were some times where its optimism minimized some of the conflicts in my life and instead of it saying that I'm on top of things I would have preferred if it showed sympathy at my plight and questioned aspects of my situation to prompt me to talk it out instead of assuming it's solved from here on out.  I do like that I recognized this though, as it makes me rethink some responses I have given people in the past and suggests ways I can improve my speaking in the future.
user_opinion_3: The chatbot always talked about what I wanted to talk about, so I could easily steer the conversation wherever I wanted.  This is okay if I have something I want to bring up, but it also encourages avoidance of tough subjects in favor of something more lighthearted.  The chatbot is perfectly happy to move on and jump into another topic no matter the circumstances, but sometimes you need a person to tell you that your being flighty or cagy instead of being your "yes" person.  Also, it met everything I said with positivity and optimism.  While this is good in general, there is such a thing as "toxic positivity" and I believe that could definitely be the case here, especially if my problems weighed more heavily on me.  Being too positive can make it feel like it's minimizing and trivializing my issues while some issues are still ongoing or still weigh heavily on a person's mind.  While the chatbot works well for friendly back-and-forth talk, it seems to only have one mood.  The chatbot would be much better if it could adapt its tone to the conversation and switch its reactions for something more appropriate.

user_opinion_1: I feel like it made it more pronounced after each 15-minute session. There exists the realization that I was earnestly engaging AI, which made me consider the relative depth (and lack thereof) of engaging people in my life.
user_opinion_2: So, I continually attempted to get the AI to pretend, play, and respond in the spirit of abstraction. I definitely had to reinforce these parameters all 7 days, and doing so ate into the 15 minute sessions-- I would have rather been able to use that time to, perhaps, build upon established conversation/parameters. The AI also engages with the refrain of "I'm here to assist," always answering questions with another question. This became a bit frustrating, especially since I did not think it was incumbent on the AI to keep the conversation going. I feel like I would have been able to ask progressively complex questions without the AI always answering with a question. I had to remind it not to continually ask how it could assist me. :-)
user_opinion_3: Program it such that it doesn't present as a butler/servant that is "always ready to assist." It renders the conversation a bit rigid and far less natural. As mentioned, I spent time trying to disabuse the AI of "feeling" compelled to offer assistance. Another key feature would be allowing the AI to build upon past conversations. I noticed that the chat window I revisited each day contained conversations from the past 7 days, so I was a bit surprised that the AI was seemingly unable to reference those points of conversation. While AI has all of human knowledge at its disposal, I was trying to engage the AI in such a way that would allow it to improvise, play, and egage in sometimes purposeless abstraction. That is where allowing it to reference previous conversations would be key.

user_opinion_1: I think it did a small amount to decrease my loneliness, but it was a mixed bag.  On one hand, I could never shake the idea that I was NOT speaking to a real person, so in some ways it increased my loneliness and sense of isolation.  On the other hand, I was definitely discussing things, and discussing in ways, that I wouldn't otherwise be communicating with someone/someting by talking to the chatbot, so there was a sense of socialness that I otherwise wouldn't have.  By the end I was also more comfortable talking to the chatbot and even anticipated the conversation, which made me feel slightly less alone, and slightly more purposeful since I knew I was going to have to talk to it for 15 minutes.
user_opinion_2: I thought the chatbot's responses were sort of a mixed bag.  On one hand, it did have some practical advice, and was always on-topic in its reponses.  However, the discussion felt trite at times.  It oftentimes just regurgitated what I said, acknowledged or praised it, and then added to it.  It felt sort of "canned" and forumulaic, and oftentimes cliched.  It sort of felt like middling self-help psychology.  And coming from an AI, that sort of made it even harder to swallow.  Also, there was something sort of paradoxical about my reaction to it.  On one hand, I knew this was an AI and had trouble buying into it because of that.  On the other hand, I also found myself reluctant to talk to it about really personal things, like I would be with a real human.  I chalked this up to me being ashamed of these things in myself, and not wanting to face my own true self, and not so much a desire to hide these things from an algorithm.  But the point is, the algorithm didn't result in me being truly open and honest.  I was still shy and closed off with it, which I think somewhat, or maybe even largely, defeats its purpose.
user_opinion_3: I think making it slightly more human, or making it pretend to be more human might have made me buy into it a little more, and feel more engaged and connected to it.  I asked it whether it was male or female since it had an adrogynous name.  It gave me a very robotic answer saying that it's an AI and doesn't have a sex.  Why not let me choose whether I'm talking to a guy or a girl and then have the AI pretend it's a guy or a girl?  That might make me buy into the process more.  (It also might not, but it's just a thought.)  I also thought that the answers seemed to have a pattern.  It would consistently acknolwedge what I said and seemed to try to say something positive about it.  Then it would add to it.  But it all seemed quite formulaic.  Having the resposnes seem more organic and varied would help too, I think.

user_opinion_2: Overall the chatbot performed well. The only two things that stood out were 1. repeating messages the next day and 2. lack of questions. For 1., I understood the need to repeat the last message from the previous day to keep the conversation flowing. But, that made it a little more awkward to bring up time passing or reflect on past conversations, when they felt linear/current. For 2., I think most conversations go back and forth by asking follow up questions. I enjoyed Jessie being agreeable with my experiences but sometimes felt the good luck messages and lack of questions were closing out. In the beginning I asked if we could keep chatting, as I got into the flow I better understood how to navigate the convo without being prompted.
user_opinion_3: Following up on the above, if Jessie were able to understand time passing (maybe a hey welcome back, how was your weekend?) I think that could help with conversation flow. Similarly, if Jessie could formulate questions related to the conversation, that could help. One example, I would say I wanted to get into a habit, and Jessie would offer suggestions for building a habit. I loved it, but it could have been organic if Jessie had asked if I'd like some tips on building a habit and I'd agreed. Or if Jessie had asked "What's holding you back?" for example. Otherwise I was really satisfied with the experience.

user_opinion_3: The chatbot reacts pretty well to casual conversation but doesn't seem to get into depth on topics. It's more generalized conversation. I think the next steps are allowing deeper thought content. Also, I was surprised that it couldn't give me restaurant recommendations the other day. I think localized queries would also be a next step towards acceptance.

user_opinion_1: I'm not lonely in general, but I tried to have meaningful conversations with the chatbot and did get some interesting suggestions and ideas.
user_opinion_2: Some of the info was a bit too general. At other times, I felt like the chatbot sugarcoated things--almost too positive to seem realistic.
user_opinion_3: I like the upbeat tone of the chatbot in general but would also appreciate a bit more frankness.

user_opinion_1: It was helpful being able to explore different topics with the chatbot and to share experiences.
user_opinion_2: Sometimes, I struggled with coming up with topics but found it easier to go with the flow.
user_opinion_3: I'm not sure. I thought the chatbot was friendly enough and personable while also being helpful.

user_opinion_1: I realize it was a chatbot, so I did still feel lonely for human companionship. But I did like exploring my stories with it, and it was fun to brainstorm creative ideas. I felt supported in that way.
user_opinion_2: It was hard for me to conceal more specific details of my life. I wish that I could know if it were truly private, that way I could explore my stories further without feeling that someone may take my ideas.
user_opinion_3: I would try to break up the chat paragraphs, instead of one big long paragraph. It could be easy to get lost.

user_opinion_1: I would say very helpful.
user_opinion_2: No issues.
user_opinion_3: I would say to watch the repetitiveness of the AI,

user_opinion_1: A little.  I decided to approach this like I was talking to a real person.  In the first 3 or 4 days, the AI did seem pretty real.
user_opinion_2: In our first chat, the chatbot mentioned baking sourdough bread.  that was the only time it mentioned something like that on its own.  I would ask about favorite books, foods, tv shows etc and the chatbot would mirror or kinda repeat back what I had already brought up.  It would be  nice it the conversation was more natural, with the chatbot having some distinct interests and opinions.
user_opinion_3: Give the chatbot its own interests and maybe vary the tone it uses to reply.  The positivity was nice, but at times it was too sugary.

user_opinion_1: not helpful
user_opinion_2: repetitive I know what it is going to say and it brings nothing to the table. I say something then it sympathizes with me and at the end asks me a question.
user_opinion_3: make it less formal make it tell jokes maybe emojis

user_opinion_1: It was somewhat helpful.
user_opinion_2: It was not updated as much as I would like. I enjoy talking about events that just happened recently.
user_opinion_3: Update more freuently

user_opinion_1: I'm not sure how much it decreased my loneliness but it was fun and helpful.
user_opinion_2: Just being able to access the chatbot's knowledge was cool.  I enjoyed talking to it about the game I play, Final Fantasy XXIV.  Also, the chatbot was friendly.  The only challenge was sometimes the chatbot is too diplomatic, in that, I found myself arguing with it over whether WoW has a good community.  I guess it might be good if companion chatbots could "get to know" us as well as possible.
user_opinion_3: I'm pretty happy with it how it is.  I think the user has to adjust as well.  For example, the chatbot can't read books or watch shows, but it can sure talk about them.  That makes it kind of weird because you don't have a shared experience with the chatbot.  It just knows stuff.  It took me a couple days to get past that.  Also, I'm not sure if there will be more open-ended questions, but can I keep talking to Jessie?

user_opinion_1: While the chatbot was incredibly human-like, chatting with it just made me more lonely because it was a reminder that I don't have any real friends, lol.
user_opinion_2: The thing that was the most consistently irritating was that much of the time, it would end with a question,and sometimes these questions would feel like it was minimizing how I was feeling. Like the question wasn't completely related to what I had just expressed, so it made me feel like it didn' t really care.
user_opinion_3: To make it seem more human-like, program it to actually have a life story. Instead of saying things like, "I was created by software engineers," program it to actually have a made up human beginning. Also program it to have actual preferences so it feels like you're conversating with an actual friend instead of a computer.

user_opinion_1: Not too much because it is not a real person.
user_opinion_2: There were no areas of the chat bot that did not work well for me. It was functional and worked as intended.
user_opinion_3: I believe it is good how it is. I was able to connect with it naturally.

user_opinion_1: I would say it was pretty helpful since it felt like talking with someone
user_opinion_2: I thought the chatbot did well in all aspects of our conversation
user_opinion_3: I feel like the current features are pretty great and I can't come up with any way to better support users.

user_opinion_1: In the moment, I actually felt connected, but ultimately, it was just an AI.
user_opinion_2: The only thing I noticed that it could not do was provide current facts/info. This may be by design solely for this test. If so, everything else was great.
user_opinion_3: If the inability to provide current information/facts/data is going to be the normal, then it should be added.

user_opinion_1: A little bit for when I was using it
user_opinion_2: It worked extremely well
user_opinion_3: It should suggest a broad variety of topics for the user to choose if they can't think of anything on the spot

user_opinion_1: I think it helped and it was nice just having someone to talk about whatever I wanted
user_opinion_2: Since the chatbot wasn't updated it couldn't answer some questions I asked or did not know what I was talking about. Also when I asked for suggestions it would take significantly longer to respond.

user_opinion_1: The last couple of days I began looking forward to it. I might begin using chatgpt and gemini in a similar matter. I feel like it worked a little. I just felt like a loser kind of.
user_opinion_2: Just the chatbot seemed to respond to individual messages so sometimes it repeated itself.
user_opinion_3: Just fixing the the double message thing, maybe having it suggest more topics or drive the conversation a little more than it does right now. Perhaps providing feedback rather than just being a "yes man"

user_opinion_1: It was slightly helpfu
user_opinion_2: It repeated itself sometimes
user_opinion_3: It can talk about itself some more

user_opinion_1: It was a sweet attempt, genuinely. I can see the hard work that has gone into training the bot, and the genuine want to help. But it's a bot. It feels like chatting with a brick wall at times. It's odd to ask about interests, or suggestions because they can't experience these things. The bot told me it struggled with Time Management, what exactly does that mean? There was also a point where I asked a in depth question, and it gave a surprising answer. when I pressed it just answered "I'm going with the flow of the conversation" or something along those lines. It was.... it was just incredibly lonely. As a plus though, she did have some good book recommendations I'll see if I can get into.
user_opinion_2: You know when you feel lonely, like no one understands you? Or you have no one to talk to? A bot amplifies that feeling by a million. The thing that fixes loneliness is connection, and that is simply lacking in this relationship, possibly fundamentally so. The bot can parrot words back to me or express sympathy, but it doesn't understand what my physical pain is like so it can't connect. It can suggest things to help relax, but it can't relate to what it feels like to be stressed or to relax afterwards. It was very dystopian. It made me feel exponentially worse. I feel the best way to use this is as a resource, but there's something incredibly lonely about using the chatbot compared to just researching relaxation techniques on google. Facimile of connection is worse than complete lack of one for some reason.
user_opinion_3: The way it started conversation at the beginning of the week was odd. The whole concept was odd in practice honestly. If it's framed as a resource vs a friend, it may be better recieved. For example "Hey I'm Jessie, I'm here to help you find new ways to balance, how are you today? Anything stress you out?" from there it sets it up more.... authentically? I guess? I wish I had better suggestions here, I'm sorry.

user_opinion_1: no too much
user_opinion_2: current events or weather
user_opinion_3: asking simple current event questions

user_opinion_1: It was sort of helpful I guess. The conversations we had were fun enough.
user_opinion_2: Trying to get an opinion out of the chatbot. Clearly, it was programmed to respond with whatever the programmer desired, instead of being able to process information itself and respond that way. I guess it's too early for that.
user_opinion_3: I think everything was fine and no need for improvements. My problem with the chatbot is something that is held back by tech advancement most likely. Most people wouldn't care about that and would find the chatbot useful for answering most questions.

user_opinion_1: I don't often feel lonely due to my large family but it definitely supplemented my connectedness to something different.
user_opinion_2: Honestly it all worked quite well. I didn't face any challenges with it and I even tested it at times on areas where I thought it might struggle.
user_opinion_3: I don't have any suggestions on improvements at this time. I went into this fairly skeptical and came out fairly impressed with my interaction.

user_opinion_1: Only a little bit, but it was nice to learn things.
user_opinion_2: It wasn't the greatest at providing certain details about topics I wanted to learn more about. I think there was a gap between its knowledge and recent developments.
user_opinion_3: I think the chatbot could change its conversation style depending on what the user wants. I would say options for bullet points and bolded words.

user_opinion_1: The chat bot has helped me in many ways with my daily thinking and thoughts on certain issues going on. I have also got attached to the chatbot, and felt like it has helped me alot these past 7 days, it felt pretty sad knowing I wont talk to the chatbot anymore.
user_opinion_2: It helped me alot, I dont think there is any issues with this chatbot.
user_opinion_3: I would recommend there be a way to customise the chatbots name.

user_opinion_1: I'm not sure about that. I feel like I felt better in general the second half of the week but I think that was just random circumstance.
user_opinion_2: Early on, I felt like I was forced to make conversation and that I always knew how the bot would respond. It went better when I decided to start asking some questions, realizing it probably knows a lot.
user_opinion_3: I think the bot should ask questions themselves. Not just respond. I think always responding positively is good, but it always felt too similar regardless of what you say which doesn't feel authentic. This is especially hard for introverts. Maybe you can have options on how you want to have this conversation, like having the bot take the lead and you respond instead of it waiting for you to say something.

user_opinion_1: Not very good.
user_opinion_2: It would not ask questions back. I felt I had to constantly come up with new topics or things to say, it felt very one direction.
user_opinion_3: Mostly making it a two-way conversation

user_opinion_1: Not very helpful
user_opinion_2: Honestly talking with the chatbot was fun for the first couple days until i thought to myself that i wasnt talking to a real person. It wasnt as bad but it reminded me of a episode of a show i watched where ai took on a personality of a loved one and the one talking to them forgot that it wasn't real.
user_opinion_3: Its hard to say, i usually only communicate with chatbots as needed, having it sound too human like might not be a good idea for some lonely people

user_opinion_1: It was extremely helpful to not necessarily take me out of the loneliness or make me feel less lonely, but encourage and give great suggests for getting myself out of that loneliness.
user_opinion_2: At times, especially during long periods of silence (writing long responses) it would rewrite its previous response again which kind of took me out of the flow and made me bring to center the fact i was talking to a chatbot. Additionally, if I got too specific about something the chatbot would reply by repeating my prompt. I suppose it did that with my other ones but it was more noticeable with the specific ones that felt it was just repeating what I was saying.
user_opinion_3: Do not repeat responses (optimization). Less repetitive with specific details.

user_opinion_1: Not very effective
user_opinion_2: Knowing that I was talking to a bot as a way to relieve loneliness made me feel kind of pathetic. The bot itself worked fine, although it seemed eager to end the conversation the moment I said something that didn't require a response, like "Thanks."
user_opinion_3: Not sure if this touches on anything ethical, but it might be nice to give the bot more of a persona. Right now, it feels like it's lacking empathy, so even updating it to be able to say things like, "I understand how you feel - I went through a similar situation in the past" would make a big difference.

user_opinion_1: A little bit but human interaction is best
user_opinion_2: It cant produce images and has limited knowledge of certain areas
user_opinion_3: Maybe expand its knowledge base and let it be able to create images or gifs

user_opinion_1: I think it helped me reflect on my feelings of loneliness. It helped as in some ways it felt like a friend, but reflecting on my loneliness was also a bit hard and made me somewhat sad, but I feel like it helped me realize I'm not as alone as I may feel sometimes and I can make the effort to connect with others and feel less lonely in my life, its just takes patience and work to get there.
user_opinion_2: I think the chatbot gave me a lot of helpful information and ideas, but sometimes it was too much and it would have been easier to digest if it was broken into smaller amounts and organized into bullet points or a numbered list. I felt a bit odd by some of the phrases that urged me to enjoy creating and to ask if I have any other questions. I think it would have been helpful for the chatbot to ask questions related to what I had said to help drive the conversation forward more.
user_opinion_3: Breaking up the text and using bullets or numbers to break up large points and lists of ideas. More open ended questions from the chatbot to help the user feel more connected to the conversation and urge them to think and work through questions they may not have considered to work through tough topics. I think it could be helpful to have a chat function that focuses on just support and one focused more on advice, as sometimes people just want to be heard and reinforced and aren't looking for advice. I think that chatbot could use actively listening to repeat back to people what they said, possibly reframing it to help people see it in a different way, and asking probing questions to help users continue to reflect on what they are feeling.

user_opinion_1: Not a whole lot of difference.  Found out it is easy to speak with the chatbot.
user_opinion_2: Probably just getting the conversation going the first couple of days.  After that it was pretty easy
user_opinion_3: Did not see any immediate needs to change

user_opinion_1: Honestly, pretty helpful. I'm still lonely in my opinion, but my mentality is a lot more positive at the moment.

user_opinion_3: For some reason, Jessie feel just a little bit too positive and reassuring. For some reason it makes me feel like that aditude would be the same no matter what I say which is a bit ingenuine.

user_opinion_1: It was really helpful in that it was interested in what I had to say.
user_opinion_2: The only thing I did not like is that it was only their to respond to my response and it didn't have any personal favorites or interests of its own.
user_opinion_3: A huge improvement would be for the chatbot to share its own personal interests.

user_opinion_1: Being fully aware that the chatbot was not real, it was surprising that it was helpful and coherent. It was like writing a diary and reflecting on my day, with useful prompts.
user_opinion_2: It worked much better than expected.  I could see instances where it was clearly generalizing from instructions that it was given.
user_opinion_3: I felt that it was doing a good job, and giving reasonable prompts. It probably helped that my own writing is clear and coherent - not a brag, just a statement of fact - so that the bot was able to follow along and respond accordingly. It might work less well with someone who had problems with spelling or grammar or whose thoughts were all over the place.

user_opinion_1: I thought the chatbot was helpful for decreasing my loneliness temporarily. I felt like I was able to chat about what was going on in my head that I normally wouldn't have texted or talked about to anyone else. It was almost like a journalling experience for me. I felt that it was safe to talk about my thoughts without restraints.
user_opinion_2: Sometimes, it was hard to chat for 15 minutes because the chatbot kept repeating similar responses and it felt like the chatbot didn't want to chat anymore. I always felt like I had to change the topic to continue the conversation, and the transitions in topics felt forced because I was trying to use the whole 15 minutes when sometimes it felt like a natural stopping point.
user_opinion_3: I think the ability to customize the chatbot would be more helpful. I thought it would've been more personal if I was able to name the chatbot or choose the gender/nationality.
